evidence_quotes.quote: It's unfair that companies can take away what I bought
Overwatch 2 they just replaced the first game, i lost a game i loved and in return got something i despised.
it's a matter of being able to keep enjoying a game they love

paragraphs.text: It's my opinion that something like the goal of the "Stop destroying videogames" initiative would fit into what the "Digital Fairness Act" is trying to do. It seems unfair that companies can take away what I bought, or me not knowing when I suddenly can't enjoy what I bought anymore. For some it's a matter of being able to keep enjoying a game they love and others it's a matter of being able to keep enjoying their hobby, as not everyone is able to spend money on the newest thing to be able to keep their hobby. To me it seems akin to not being able to use your computer, at all, after Microsoft/Apple stops supporting whatever operating system you had installed. Just because they stop supporting it shouldn't mean they can take it away. On a personal note, i loved the game Overwatch but when they came out with Overwatch 2 they just replaced the first game, i lost a game i loved and in return got something i despised.
